channel.title: NPR Topics: Health
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Nurse convicted in patient's death is now a national speaker on hospital safety
item.description: RaDonda Vaught was convicted of negligent homicide after dispensing the wrong drug to a patient. She now gives speeches about hospital safety in an era of automation and artificial intelligence.
item.content:encoded:
RaDonda Vaught was convicted of negligent homicide after dispensing the wrong drug to a patient. She now gives speeches about hospital safety in an era of automation and artificial intelligence.

(Image credit: Mark Humphrey)
item.dc:creator: Blake Farmer

item.title: A little bit country, a little bit Desi: A Pakistani-American's hybrid music
item.description: On May 31, Mo Sabri makes a landmark appearance with the Nashville Symphony for an orchestral rendition of his new album, "Tennessee Desi," which blends Appalachian styles with Muslim devotional music.
item.content:encoded:
On May 31, Mo Sabri makes a landmark appearance with the Nashville Symphony for an orchestral rendition of his new album, "Tennessee Desi," which blends Appalachian styles with Muslim devotional music.

(Image credit: Mo Sabri)
item.dc:creator: Malaka Gharib

item.title: U.S. passengers flying from Ebola-affected countries rerouted
item.description: The U.S. government is responding to the Ebola outbreak in with travel restrictions. American citizens and permanent residents departing affected countries must fly into one of three U.S. airports.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. government is responding to the Ebola outbreak in with travel restrictions. American citizens and permanent residents departing affected countries must fly into one of three U.S. airports.

(Image credit: Michal Ruprecht for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: 15 years since a deadly tornado brought Joplin, Mo. together, kindness carries on
item.description: Nearly 100,000 volunteers helped the town rebuild and a spirit of community service continues to this day. Researchers studying human behavior catastrophes can bring out compassion in surprising ways.
item.content:encoded:
Nearly 100,000 volunteers helped the town rebuild and a spirit of community service continues to this day. Researchers studying human behavior catastrophes can bring out compassion in surprising ways.

(Image credit: FEMA)
item.dc:creator: Pauline Bartolone

item.title: One solution for Maine's struggling fishing industry? Give fillets away for free
item.description: Surging food costs and fuel prices are pummeling Maine's struggling groundfishing industry. But a pandemic-era program is helping to keep it afloat as inflation worsens.
item.content:encoded:
Surging food costs and fuel prices are pummeling Maine's struggling groundfishing industry. But a pandemic-era program is helping to keep it afloat as inflation worsens.

(Image credit: Ryan David Brown for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez

item.title: 40,000 people under evacuation orders after chemical tank leak in Southern California
item.description: About 40,000 people were under evacuation orders and schools shut down Friday in Southern California after a storage tank continued to leak a hazardous chemical that officials said could rupture or explode.
item.content:encoded:
About 40,000 people were under evacuation orders and schools shut down Friday in Southern California after a storage tank continued to leak a hazardous chemical that officials said could rupture or explode.

(Image credit: Jeff Gritchen/The Orange County Register)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: 'We'll never know if he could have been saved.' The gaps in Trump's rural health fund
item.description: Republicans promise that $50 billion in new health funding will help rural America. But it's not expected to aid the years-long effort in North Carolina's Martin County to reopen its only hospital.
item.content:encoded:
Republicans promise that $50 billion in new health funding will help rural America. But it's not expected to aid the years-long effort in North Carolina's Martin County to reopen its only hospital.

(Image credit: Sarah Jane Tribble)
item.dc:creator: Sarah Jane Tribble

item.title: Eli Lilly says its next gen obesity drug is more powerful than Zepbound and Wegovy
item.description: Eli Lilly announced clinical trial results for retatrutide, its next gen obesity drug. The drug helped study participants lose 70 lbs over 80 weeks, making it more powerful than Zepbound and Wegovy.
item.content:encoded: Eli Lilly announced clinical trial results for retatrutide, its next gen obesity drug. The drug helped study participants lose 70 lbs over 80 weeks, making it more powerful than Zepbound and Wegovy.
item.dc:creator: Sydney Lupkin

item.title: A nurse found guilty of negligent homicide is now a a sought-after speaker
item.description: After accidentally dispensing the wrong drug to a patient, former Tennessee nurse RaDonda Vaught was convicted of negligent homicide in 2022. She's now found redemption on the speaking circuit.
item.content:encoded: After accidentally dispensing the wrong drug to a patient, former Tennessee nurse RaDonda Vaught was convicted of negligent homicide in 2022. She's now found redemption on the speaking circuit.
item.dc:creator: Blake Farmer

item.title: American passenger feels 'betrayed' by federal order to stay in hantavirus quarantine
item.description: Two passengers from the cruise ship that had a hantavirus outbreak were blocked by the federal government from leaving the quarantine unit. One says she is being detained against her will.
item.content:encoded:
Two passengers from the cruise ship that had a hantavirus outbreak were blocked by the federal government from leaving the quarantine unit. One says she is being detained against her will.

(Image credit: Dylan Widger)
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein
